notes.eibor: CONFIRMED at 3.972% as of July 3 2026 (CBUAE tool); BREAKS 206-consecutive-day streak at 3.8155%; +15.65 bps increase; CBUAE base rate 3.65% unchanged (June 30); EIBOR-base spread widened to 32.2 bps (above typical 10-30 bps range)
notes.brent: July 9 intraday $77.17-$78.80 (multiple web sources); used $77.50 midpoint for calculations; FRED July 6 close $69.56; ceasefire collapse July 8 drove +4-6% surge; Trump declared ceasefire over at NATO summit Turkey; US launched strikes on 80+ Iran targets after ships struck in Hormuz
notes.wti: July 9 web-sourced $74.26-$75.00; used $74.63 midpoint; FRED July 6 close $69.60
notes.hormuz: CEASEFIRE COLLAPSED July 8 2026. Iranian-linked forces struck 3 vessels in Hormuz. US launched retaliatory strikes on 80+ targets inside Iran. Trump declared ceasefire 'over' at NATO summit Turkey. Brent surged 4-6% to $78-79. New scenario framework: Escalation contained $75-82 (50%), Rapid de-escalation $68-74 (20%), Severe escalation $85-95+ (30%). Prior Doha accord base case (65%) superseded.
notes.iran_deal_status: Ceasefire OVER as of July 8 2026. Doha talks ended with no breakthrough (July 1 recap). Article 5 demining deadline July 17 now moot. New escalation phase. Watch: any back-channel resumption via Oman; NATO summit Turkey outcomes; Lloyd's insurance suspension announcements.

notes.us_cpi: May 2026: 4.2% YoY (energy shock, 23.5% energy surge); core 2.9%; next release: July 14 2026 (June data) — CRITICAL watch
notes.us_pce: May 2026 BEA confirmed June 25: core 3.4% YoY (HOT); next release: July 30 2026 (June PCE)
notes.us_10yr: 4.55% (FRED July 7, +7bps from 4.48% July 6); web-confirmed 4.57% July 9 (Trading Economics); breakeven inflation 2.25% (FRED July 8, +3bps from 2.22%); real yield ~2.32%; highest since energy shock peak

claude_md_patches_applied.patch: EIBOR estimation basis must appear as first line of calculation code block (not only in narrative). Sources section is mandatory with vintage dates for all FRED series. Keep brief body under 3,500 words so Sources/Methodology always fit.

Use **Key number:** (bold, lowercase) and **Watch list — next 72 hours:** (bold) — not ### headings. Sources and Methodology are mandatory final sections; compress Type C before omitting them. Do not generate YAML frontmatter — header must use bold markdown key-value pairs after plain --- HR.

EIBOR estimation chain must be first line of calculation block: 'EIBOR source: estimated (state.json baseline X.XX%; CBUAE base rate X.XX% confirmed [date]; N consecutive days)'. Stale FRED series must be disclosed by name with vintage dates in body AND Methodology. Methodology section is mandatory in every brief.

Always run all four required calculations (EIBOR sensitivity, oil fiscal USD+AED, petrochemical pass-through, Op300bn run-rate) in every brief including same-day refreshes. Corrected Op300bn method: (200-133)/(300-133)=40.1% of gap, not 200/300=66.7%.
